headline: No significant vulnerabilities found on cleanapp.example.com; TLS and security header controls are well-configured.
narrative:
The passive scan of cleanapp.example.com found no High, Medium, or Low severity vulnerabilities. TLS configuration is strong: TLS 1.3 and 1.2 are the only negotiated protocols, legacy cipher suites are absent, and the certificate chain is valid. Security response headers — including HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and Content Security Policy — are present and correctly configured.

The single Informational finding reflects standard public DNS and certificate records that are visible to any external observer. This is expected for any public-facing application and does not represent a meaningful risk, though it is noted for completeness in the attack-surface record.

Overall, this application presents a well-maintained security baseline. No immediate remediation is required. The recommended actions below are optional hardening steps that would further reduce residual risk.
key_findings: TLS configuration is current: TLS 1.3 and 1.2 only, no legacy protocols or weak cipher suites detected.
All key HTTP security headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options, CSP) are present and properly configured.
Passive recon found only standard public DNS and certificate records — no unexpected infrastructure exposure.
recommended_actions: Maintain current TLS and header configuration through patch cycles and periodic re-scanning.
Review DNS and certificate transparency logs periodically to detect any unexpected subdomain or certificate issuance.
